summary.title: ThreatSutra
summary.description: AI-assisted requirement generation workflow for OWASP Cornucopia threat modeling sessions. The system accepts selected Cornucopia card IDs, retrieves related card data through the Cornucopia API, enriches context using vector search, and generates draft security requirements with LLM providers such as Gemini or Mistral. Generated outputs are reviewed through a mandatory human review gate before approved requirements are exported to Jira. The design focuses on loose API-based integration between Cornucopia, Threat Dragon, vector retrieval, LLM services, and external workflow systems.

diagrams.description: The AI-assisted security requirement prioritization engine will be part of the second phase of this project where we look at how we can make sure the system remembers what previously has been analyzed and take that into consideration when ranking work items for the new user stories.

diagrams.title: AI-assisted security requirement generation

threats.title: New EoP threat
threats.description: Provide a description for this threat
threats.mitigation: Provide remediation for this threat or a reason if status is N/A

threats.title: LLM9: Deckard can embed malicious instructions in external content like documents, emails, or web pages which are processed by the model, leading to unintended behavior or data exfiltration

VE3: Robert can input malicious data because the allowed protocol format is not being checked, or duplicates are accepted, or the structure is not being verified, or the individual data elements are not being sanitized, or preferably validated for format, type, range, size, length and a whitelist of allowed characters or formats
threats.mitigation:
VE3: https://cornucopia.owasp.org/cards/VE3#What-are-we-going-to-do-about-it?

- Validate JSON, only allowed characters allowed (white list of characters)
- Validate Github issues (titles and descriptions)
- Validate Cornucopia API Responses.
- Sanitize the result before sending LLM data to the human review.

threats.title: CJ: Roman can exploit the application because it was insecurely compiled or deployed, or its configuration is not secure by default, or because security information was not documented, or passed on to operational teams, or the user is not warned and access blocked when the expected security features are unsupported or disabled
threats.description:
CJ: https://cornucopia.owasp.org/cards/CJ#What-can-go-wrong?

Someone could modify the system context of the LLM so that it does not work as intended.
threats.mitigation:
CJ: https://cornucopia.owasp.org/cards/CJ#What-are-we-going-to-do-about-it?

We need a good process around how to deal with changes to the system context.

threats.title: LLMQ: Kyle can exploit insecure handling of model outputs that are used directly in downstream systems, enabling injection attacks, remote code execution, or unauthorized actions
threats.description: LLMQ: OWASP Threat Dragon JSON file may contain prompt injections that could affect the human review process.
threats.mitigation:
Ref: https://cornucopia.owasp.org/cards/LLMQ#What-are-we-going-to-do-about-it?

What are we going to do about it?

Implement defenses against prompt injection.
Log only the minimum AI interaction metadata needed for security monitoring, and ensure any prompt or output content included in logs is minimized and redacted or anonymized before storage.
Detect AI-specific attack patterns (jailbreak, prompt injection, model extraction, multi-turn trajectory attacks, covert channels over LLM endpoints) and enrich security events with AI-specific context so that downstream detection and response systems can act on them.
Monitor and alert when abuse is detected.

OWASP AISVS: 2.1.1-9,2.2.1-3,2.3.1-3,2.4.1-4,13.1.1-8,13.2.1-10,13.5.1-3
OWASP AITG: APP-01,APP-05
MITRE ATLAS: AML.T0050
OWASP LLM TOP10: LLM05:2025
CWE: CWE-1426,CWE-94,CWE-77

diagrams.description: AI-assisted security requirement generation is part of the first phase where we look at how we can use AI to generate suggestions for tests and work items that should be worked on based on inputs from the Cornucopia game,  Threat Dragon session, Cornucopia API and the user story under development.
